events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the required assessment directly to `/app/answer.json`. The condition is a service equipment defect at the meter interface after inspection, so I’m treating it as a failed installed condition with elevated electrical and fire risk.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"tested/inspected\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\", \"worn\", \"degraded\", \"failed\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 95,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.9,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Meter jaws are burned or corroded, indicating defective service metering contact surfaces.\",
    \"Damaged meter jaws can cause high-resistance connections, overheating, arcing, loss of service continuity, and fire hazard.\",
    \"Service entrance metering equipment is not acceptable for continued use or approval in this condition.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Do not approve or place the service equipment in service in this condition.\",
    \"Coordinate utility disconnect/removal of the meter before repair or replacement.\",
    \"Replace the damaged meter socket or affected metering components per utility and manufacturer requirements.\",
    \"Inspect service conductors, terminations, socket enclosure, and downstream service equipment for heat or corrosion damage before re-energizing.\",
    \"Verify proper torque, clean contact surfaces, listing, labeling, and utility approval before final acceptance.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Burned or corroded meter jaws are defective current-carrying service equipment parts. At the tested/inspected stage, this is not a workmanship tolerance issue; it is a hazardous installed condition that fails inspection because service equipment must be suitable, intact, and installed/maintained according to its listing, labeling, manufacturer instructions, utility requirements, and NEC service-entrance rules.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NEC Article 230 - Services, service conductors and service equipment requirements\",
    \"NEC 230.66 - Service equipment listed and marked suitable for use as service equipment\",
    \"NEC 110.3(B) - Listed or labeled equipment installed and used in accordance with instructions\",
    \"NEC 110.12 - Mechanical execution of work; equipment free from damage that impairs safe operation\",
    \"NEC 110.14 - Electrical connections and terminations\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json
python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json"
→ /app
